Title: KENNEDY SPACE CENTER, FLA. - In the Columbia Debris Hangar,  Shuttle Launch Director Mike Leinbach  points to some of the debris as he explains to the media about activities that have taken place since the Columbia accident on Feb. 1, 2003.  STS-107 debris recovery and reconstruction operations are winding down.  To date, nearly 84,000 pieces of debris have been recovered and sent to KSC. That represents about 38 percent of the dry weight of Columbia, equaling almost 85,000 pounds.
CreditLine: NASA Image and Video Library (public domain)
